name: ExtensionNutritionProduct_Instance
title: R5: One or several physical instances or occurrences of the nutrition product (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `NutritionProduct.instance` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`NutritionProduct.instance` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `NutritionProduct.instance` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `NutritionProduct.instance` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionProduct` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionProduct.instance` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: An identifier that supports traceability to the event during which material in this product from one or more biological entities was obtained or pooled (new)
element.comment:
Element `NutritionProduct.instance.biologicalSourceEvent` is part of an existing definition because parent element `NutritionProduct.instance` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `NutritionProduct.instance.biologicalSourceEvent` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionProduct` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionProduct.instance.biologicalSourceEvent` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Necessary to support mandatory requirements for traceability from donor/source to recipient and vice versa, while also satisfying donor anonymity requirements. The element is defined consistently across BiologicallyDerivedProduct, NutritionProduct, and Device.  The identifier references an event that links to a single biological entity such as a blood donor, or to multiple biological entities (e.g. when the product is an embryo or a pooled platelet product).  A single biologicalSourceEvent identifier may appear on multiple products of many types derived from a single donation event or source extraction.  As an example, a single donation event may provide 2 kidneys and a liver for organ transplantation, 2 corneas for eye surgery, heart valves and arterial tissue for cardiovascular surgery, multiple skin grafts, tendons, multiple shaped bone grafts and a large number of bone putty/paste products; and each of them may be assigned to the same biological source event identifier.
element.requirements:
Element `NutritionProduct.instance.biologicalSourceEvent` is part of an existing definition because parent element `NutritionProduct.instance` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `NutritionProduct.instance.biologicalSourceEvent` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionProduct` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionProduct.instance.biologicalSourceEvent` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: An identifier that supports traceability to the event during which material in this product from one or more biological entities was obtained or pooled
element.definition: An identifier that supports traceability to the event during which material in this product from one or more biological entities was obtained or pooled.
element.comment: Necessary to support mandatory requirements for traceability from donor/source to recipient and vice versa, while also satisfying donor anonymity requirements. The element is defined consistently across BiologicallyDerivedProduct, NutritionProduct, and Device. A single biologicalSourceEvent identifier may appear on multiple products of many types derived from a single donation event or source extraction.  As an example, a single donation event may provide 2 kidneys and a liver for organ transplantation, 2 corneas for eye surgery, heart valves and arterial tissue for cardiovascular surgery, multiple skin grafts, tendons, multiple shaped bone grafts and a large number of bone putty/paste products; and each of them may be assigned to the same biological source event identifier.
